title: AI-Augmented Reconnaissance & OSINT

modules.title: Reconnaissance in the AI Era
modules.sub: How AI is reshaping the modern recon workflow
modules.description: Learners survey how LLMs and AI tooling have changed the reconnaissance phase of penetration testing. They map traditional recon stages against AI-augmented equivalents and identify where automation adds speed without sacrificing analyst judgment.

modules.title: Passive OSINT with LLMs
modules.sub: Extracting open-source intelligence without touching the target
modules.description: Learners use LLMs to query, summarize, and cross-reference publicly available data sources while maintaining a passive footprint. They practice prompt engineering techniques designed to surface actionable intelligence from unstructured web content.

modules.title: Attack-Surface Mapping at Scale
modules.sub: Building a comprehensive picture of an organization's exposure
modules.description: Learners apply AI-assisted tools to enumerate domains, IP ranges, exposed services, and cloud assets across a target organization. They make triage decisions about which discovered assets represent the highest-priority entry points.

modules.title: Email and Identity Harvesting
modules.sub: Finding and validating personnel data for targeted engagement
modules.description: Learners use AI tooling to collect, deduplicate, and validate email addresses, employee names, and role data from public sources. They evaluate the quality and confidence of harvested identity records before passing them to downstream attack planning.

modules.title: Tech-Stack Fingerprinting with AI
modules.sub: Inferring target technology choices from observable signals
modules.description: Learners feed observable artifacts — HTTP headers, job postings, certificate data, and code repositories — into LLM-assisted pipelines to infer a target's technology stack. They produce a structured fingerprint that links identified technologies to known vulnerability classes.

modules.title: From Recon to Target List
modules.sub: Synthesizing raw findings into a prioritized attack list
modules.description: Learners consolidate outputs from prior modules into a ranked target list by applying AI-assisted scoring against risk and exploitability criteria. They practice the analyst decision-making required to move from noisy data to a focused, justified engagement scope.

modules.title: Operational Security and Detection Risk
modules.sub: Keeping AI-assisted recon hidden from defensive teams
modules.description: Learners assess which AI-augmented recon activities generate detectable signals and apply operational security controls to reduce exposure. They audit a simulated recon workflow and redesign query patterns, tooling choices, and timing to lower detection risk.

modules.title: Reporting Recon Findings
modules.sub: Communicating recon results clearly to technical and business stakeholders
modules.description: Learners use LLM assistance to structure, draft, and refine a recon findings report that meets professional penetration testing standards. They make editorial decisions about evidence selection, risk language, and remediation guidance to produce a report that drives client action.

synopsis: Modern penetration testers use LLMs and AI tooling to accelerate reconnaissance — OSINT, attack-surface mapping, target enumeration — without losing analyst judgment. This course teaches the workflow end-to-end with hands-on labs.
